content.problemStatement:
Let's define a function P(x,y)=(x−y)2P(x, y) = (x-y)^2P(x,y)=(x−y)2. For given real numbers aaa and bbb, let's define a polynomial Qa,b(x)=P(P(P(x,a),a−b),x−b)Q_{a,b}(x) = P(P(P(x, a), a-b), x-b)Qa,b​(x)=P(P(P(x,a),a−b),x−b).

Next, let's define a function F(a,b)F(a, b)F(a,b):


If Qa,b(x)Q_{a,b}(x)Qa,b​(x) has no real roots, F(a,b)=0F(a, b) = 0F(a,b)=0.
Otherwise, let rrr be the largest real root of Qa,b(x)Q_{a,b}(x)Qa,b​(x).
If rrr is rational, it can be represented by a fraction r=pqr = \frac{p}{q}r=qp​, where ppp and qqq are coprime integers and q>0q \gt 0q>0. Then, F(a,b)=p+qF(a, b) = p + qF(a,b)=p+q.
If rrr is irrational, it can be proved that in this case, it can be represented in the form r=p+zqr = \frac{p + \sqrt{z}}{q}r=qp+z​​, where ppp, qqq and zzz are integers, q>0q \gt 0q>0 and z>0z \gt 0z>0. Choose the representation with the smallest value of qqq (it is guaranteed to be unique in this case). Then, F(a,b)=p+z+qF(a, b) = p + z + qF(a,b)=p+z+q.
You are given a sequence of integers c1,c2,…,cNc_1, c_2, \ldots, c_Nc1​,c2​,…,cN​. Find ∑i=1N∑j=1NF(ci,cj)\sum_{i=1}^N \sum_{j=1}^N F(c_i, c_j)∑i=1N​∑j=1N​F(ci​,cj​).
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105
`Explanation**Example case 1:** Q0,0(x)=x4−xQ_{0,0}(x) = x^4-xQ0,0​(x)=x4−x, so the largest real root is r=11r = \frac{1}{1}r=11​, F(0,0)=1+1=2F(0, 0) = 1+1 = 2F(0,0)=1+1=2 and the answer is 222.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers c1,c2,…,cNc_1, c_2, \ldots, c_Nc1​,c2​,…,cN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the sum of F(ci,cj)F(c_i, c_j)F(ci​,cj​).
